content.text:
```mdl
@ Contract: CNDA – Confidentiality and Non‑Disclosure Agreement
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_10_nda_20

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Types
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Article 2 – Definitions
type Party =
    | Disclosing(unit)   # the party that discloses Confidential Information
    | Receiving(unit)   # the party that receives Confidential Information

@ Article 2 – Confidential Information
type ConfidentialInfo = {
    is_marked_confidential : bool,   # true when the information is marked “Confidential”
    is_residual            : bool,   # true when the information is “Residual Information” (Article 3.11)
    is_returned            : bool,   # true after the Receiving Party has returned the information (Article 2.07)
    is_destroyed           : bool,   # true after the Receiving Party has destroyed the information (Article 2.07)
    is_disclosed_to_third  : bool,   # true if the information has been disclosed to a non‑representative third party
    retained_by            : Party   # which party currently holds the information
}

@ Article 2 – Representatives (people who may receive Confidential Information)
type Representative = {
    name            : string,
    has_need_to_know: bool   # true when the representative is permitted to receive the information
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entities (public model objects)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Parties to the Agreement
entity disclosing_party : Party
entity receiving_party : Party

@ The piece of Confidential Information that is the subject of the CNDA
entity confidential_info : ConfidentialInfo

@ A representative of the Receiving Party (used in the “need‑to‑know” test)
entity representative : Representative

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rules – deontic requirements (O = obligation, F = prohibition, P = permission)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Article 2.03 – “Kept in Confidence”
rule O maintain_confidential_care:
    -- The Receiving Party must use at least a reasonable degree of care.
    -- We model this as a prohibition on careless handling (i.e. the
    -- information must not be marked as “careless” – a placeholder field).
    true always   # placeholder – actual care level is out of scope for this model

@ Article 2.04 – “No Benefit, Restricted Use”
rule F derive_benefit_from_confidential:
    confidential_info.is_marked_confidential and
    confidential_info.retained_by = Receiving
    implies not confidential_info.is_disclosed_to_third always

@ Article 2.05 – “No Disclosure”
rule F disclose_to_third_party:
    confidential_info.is_marked_confidential and
    confidential_info.retained_by = Receiving
    implies not confidential_info.is_disclosed_to_third always

@ Article 2.06 – “Disclosure Required by Law”
rule P disclose_under_legal_process:
    confidential_info.is_marked_confidential and
    confidential_info.retained_by = Receiving
    implies (confidential_info.is_disclosed_to_third or true) eventually   -- permission only when required by law

@ Article 2.07 – “Return of Confidential Information”
rule O return_and_destroy:
    confidential_info.is_marked_confidential and
    confidential_info.retained_by = Receiving
    implies (confidential_info.is_returned and confidential_info.is_destroyed) always

@ Article 3.11 – “Residual Information”
rule P use_residual_information:
    -- The Receiving Party is permitted to use Residual Information even after
    -- the return or destruction of the original Confidential Information.
    confidential_info.is_residual and
    confidential_info.retained_by = Receiving always

@ Article 3.11 – “Retention of Residual Information after Return/Destruction”
rule P retain_residual_after_return:
    confidential_info.is_residual and
    confidential_info.is_returned and
    confidential_info.is_destroyed and
    confidential_info.retained_by = Receiving always

@ Article 3.08 – “Responsibility for Others”
rule O responsible_for_representatives:
    -- The Receiving Party is responsible for any breach caused by its representatives.
    representative.has_need_to_know implies true always   -- placeholder: ensures representatives are known

@ Article 3.09 – “Termination”
rule O confidentiality_survives_termination:
    -- Confidentiality obligations survive termination for three years.
    true always   -- temporal dimension (3‑year survival) is not modeled explicitly here

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# End of module
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
```
